title: Kickxia elatine
taxonomy: Kickxia elatine is classified within the family Plantaginaceae, and the genus Kickxia. It belongs to the order Lamiales and the class Magnoliopsida. The species elatine is part of the kingdom Plantae. As a member of the family Plantaginaceae, Kickxia elatine shares characteristics with other species in this group. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and evolutionary relationships.

intro: Kickxia elatine, also known as small bugle or small bugloss, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The bioactive constituents of Kickxia elatine include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Kickxia elatine is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, particularly those related to the respiratory system.
